narrative.headline: 2022 Subaru Outback: cracked windshields dominate the complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 451 complaints with NHTSA. Visibility/Wiper issues, largely cracking windshields, make up more than half of them (56%) and report no crashes, fires, or injuries. The electrical system (10%) and engine (4%) are reported far less often but account for most of the fires, with 3 each. The forward collision avoidance system (5%) is tied to 3 crashes. No deaths or injuries are reported in any area.

issues.title: Visibility/Wiper
issues.blurb: By far the biggest complaint: 253 reports (56%), commonly cracking windshields. No crashes, fires, or injuries reported, but expect possible windshield replacements.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 46 reports (10%), including 3 fires and 1 crash. The fire count makes this worth checking carefully.

issues.title: Visibility
issues.blurb: 26 reports (6%), again mostly cracking windshields. No crashes, fires, or injuries reported.

issues.title: Forward Collision Avoidance
issues.blurb: 23 reports (5%), tied to 3 crashes. Owners describe the automatic braking activating with no obstacle present.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Fewest reports at 18 (4%), but linked to 3 fires and 1 crash. Watch for loss of power or braking and steering assist.
